text: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the U.S. and Israel have bombed Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant four times. He warned that radioactive fallout would end life in Gulf Arab capitals, not Tehran. He added that attacks on Iran’s petrochemical facilities also convey real objectives. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The Israeli military issued an evacuation warning to residents of the city of Tyre and the areas of Hamadiya, Zaqoq al‑Mufdi and Burj al‑Shamali in southern Lebanon. Residents were called to leave their homes immediately according to the map and move north of the Zahrani River. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Iran’s FM Araghchi to Pakistan: “What we care about are the terms of a conclusive and lasting END to the illegal war that is imposed on US.” Pakistan seeks to hold US-Iran talks, however the 15 point-proposal from US is unreasonable.
🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 ⚡ Israel claimed responsibility for the attacks, despite Trump's request to avoid such attacks, which would lead to Iranian retaliation.

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The Israeli military announced that they have completed an additional wave of strikes in Beirut, targeting facilities used by the Lebanon Corps of Iran’s Quds Force. The Lebanon Corps serves as a alleged link between Hezbollah and the Iranian regime and supports Hezbollah’s military capabilities. Two alleged headquarters of the Islamic Jihad group, used by its leadership to coordinate with Hezbollah and plan attacks against Israel, were also struck. The Israeli Navy additionally struck an alleged Hezbollah observation post. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 🇮🇱 ⚡ Israeli attacks on a major petrochemical plant in Bandar-e Mahshahr, southwest Iran. There have also been attacks on oil-rich cities including Abadan, Khorramshahr, and Mehran near the Iran-Iraq border.

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇺🇳 The semi-official Tasnim News Agency reported a new attack targeting the vicinity of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant. The International Atomic Energy Agency said it had received no reports of any increase in radiation levels at the facility following the strike. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 ⚔️ 🇮🇷 U.S. Central Command hasn’t posted anything in nearly 24 hours, and even the last two posts were just photos, with no real updates. The missing F-15E crew member (still no confirmation whether it’s the pilot or the WSO) has not been located, and Iran has not reported any capture. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Magen David Adom announced that four people were lightly injured as a result of an Iranian ballistic missile attack on central Israel this morning. A 24‑year‑old man suffered smoke inhalation, two men in their twenties were hit by glass shards, and another man was injured by a blast. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 The governor of Khuzestan Province said that US‑Israeli strikes hit several petrochemical units in the province, causing damage. Firefighting operations were carried out quickly. The smoke spreading in the air is from the release of flare gases and is not poisonous to the public. All injured have been treated and there is no cause for concern. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 The IDF announced that during the night, an Israeli attack helicopter mistakenly dropped munition in an open area in northern Israel due to "human error." Sappers are working to collect the munition. There are no casualties, and the incident is under investigation. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 WSJ : U.S. President Trump’s fiscal 2027 budget outline calls for a 42% increase in defence spending and a 10% reduction in non‑defence outlays, targeting renewable energy, refugee resettlement and housing initiatives. The plan extends tax cuts and adds new breaks, while raising tariffs. Despite early efforts to shrink the federal workforce and cut health and nutrition spending, the deficit remains largely unchanged, with publicly held debt approaching 100% of GDP. @wfwitness

Nuno Felix RT @BDHerzinger : It’s deeply puzzling how hard some are finding it to grasp that launching an enormously damaging war of choice after a year of personal attacks and deeply unpopular policies isn’t exactly an incentive for America’s European allies to send their kids to war. When this is over, the western part of NATO will never be the same. Spain, England, France and Italy have sold us out, as they too often have a history of doing. Eastern European nations are the heart of NATO. They spend money on defense, know how to fight and love the US. France particularly deserves fault and blame. From supporting China and Russia at the UN to denying Americans overflight rights, they’re doing what they’ve always done - showing weakness, while cutting deals with terrorists. (The reason the US has a Marine Corps and Navy is unlike France, we refused to pay a ransom to the Barbary Pirates. France is always happy to cut a deal.) Wars have unintended consequences as nations show their true colors. NATO will never be the same, and Western European weakness and acquiescence is the cause. - Ari Fleischer tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 The commander of Iran’s army air defence forces, General Ali Reza Elhami, said his forces are using new Iranian methods and equipment to chase enemy aircraft. He contributed these advancements to the alleged downing of American and Israeli jets, including two U.S. fighters in a single day. He claimed that Iranian air defence has destroyed more than 160 enemy drones, including MQ‑9s, Hermes and Lukas models, and that the new capabilities have caused confusion and astonishment among the enemy. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇵🇸🇪🇬🇶🇦🇹🇷🇺🇸🇮🇱 A Hamas delegation headed by Gaza chief Khalil al‑Hayya arrived in Cairo to follow up on the implementation of the ceasefire agreement. The delegation held meetings with mediators from Egypt, Qatar and Turkey, and is scheduled to hold national…" ⚡️ 🇵🇸 🇮🇱 🇪🇬 🇺🇳 Asharq Al-Awsat: The Hamas delegation led by Khalil al‑Hayya met in Cairo with Nikolay Mladenov, the senior representative of the Gaza Strip to the Peace Council, to discuss the group’s disarmament. Hamas demands the withdrawal of Israeli forces from Gaza, the deployment of international stabilisation forces, and the establishment of a police force before any implementation of disarmament. The group believes that any violation would create a significant security vacuum. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Lebanon’s Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri said Lebanon has an interest in exiting the war and reaching a ceasefire, but no interest in tying its fate to the confrontation between Iran, the U.S. and Israel. The preferred option is for Lebanon to negotiate independently, but Israeli political stances show no willingness for talks. Any negotiation would require a mediator, though Mitri said he does not know who that mediator is. Regarding Israeli Defence Minister Katz’s announcement of a security zone up to the Litani River and the destruction of border villages, Mitri said Israel is doing what it says, and that this action is ongoing. He added that Lebanon has become one of the arenas of confrontation between Israel, the U.S. and Iran. @wfwitness

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 Libyan Training Ground of the GUR 📝 Why does Kyiv need a base in the Maghreb's "gray zone"? French media confirmed the presence of Ukrainian military personnel on Libyan territory and their responsibility for the attack on the tanker Arctic Metagaz . ❓ What other potential threats to Russia could these "instructors" pose? ➡️ It is clear that one of the objectives is to counter the African Corps of the Russian Defense Ministry directly in Libya . First and foremost, this involves intelligence and, to the extent possible, support for southern militants in their struggle against Haftar's army. ©️ Western Libya is a "black hole" with well-established smuggling routes. This is where the GUR can train personnel for Malian separatists and radical Islamists. The Tripoli-northern Mali connection has been operating for a long time, and given the Ukrainian "experience" in smuggling, contacts could expand to include supplies of scarce drones and training of militants. Western media identify the key figure in the agreement on the presence of so-called Ukrainian military personnel as the Ukrainian attaché in Algiers, General Payuk. The creation of a Ukrainian enclave near the border represents an attempt to drive a wedge into the military-technical cooperation between Moscow and Algiers leadership. 🖍 At the same time, their appearance aligns well with the anti-Russian "Libyan Corps" project, which is supposed to unite the two Libyan poles under American wings. ❗️ For the so-called Ukrainian authorities, Libya represents not so much an "opportunity to poke" Russia as a chance to prove their "usefulness" to the West as a cheap proxy tool, similar to Mozambique. As long as GUR instructors are sitting in Tripoli and Misrata, any Russian cargo in the region—from oil to food—remains in the crosshairs. High-resolution map English version #Libya #Russia #Ukraine 💀 @rybar_africa — your guide to the world of African madness 💸 Support us Original msg

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s ambassador to Egypt, Mojtaba Ferdowsi-Pour, outlined Tehran’s conditions for ending the war: A complete halt to hostilities, guarantees that attacks will not resume, compensation and the lifting of all sanctions. He accused the U.S. of making a blatant strategic mistake by striking during ongoing negotiations. On the nuclear issue, the ambassador said Iran’s position has not changed. It will not agree to permanently stop uranium enrichment on its soil, calling it the right of the Iranian people. The only proposal before the war was an Iranian offer to reduce enrichment below 60% inside Iran, not abroad, the ambassador added. @wfwitness

France is ramping up its military for future warfare by massively expanding drone and missile stockpiles by 2030. The plan includes a 400% increase in drones, 30% more missiles, and a $9 billion investment focused on weapons like guided bombs and air-defense systems.

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 U.S. / Israeli airstrikes targeted petrochemical plants in the Ahvaz area of southwestern Iran, near the Persian Gulf coast. Iranian media described the site as one of the most vital centers for the country’s oil and petrochemical industries. @wfwitness

German men aged 17–45 now must obtain permission before staying outside Germany for more than 3 months under the military service law. They are expected to notify/apply through a Bundeswehr career center before leaving long-term.

Iran has approved the passage of ships carrying essential goods and humanitarian supplies through the Strait of Hormuz to Iranian ports or nearby waters, under specific protocols. Businesses are now allowed to resume sending these shipments. Source: Tasnim
